qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated stored cross-site scripting via SVG file upload
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same vulnerable endpoint (POST /api/upload), same root cause (missing authentication middleware - no secureFnc), same code location (server/api/projects/index.js:193), and same vulnerability class (unrestricted file upload, CWE-434). The finding demonstrates the exact vulnerability described in the advisory: unauthenticated file upload enabling malicious content injection.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated remote code execution via script test execution endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable endpoint (POST /api/runscript vs POST /api/upload), different root cause (default security disabled vs missing authentication), and different code location. While this is a valid security issue in the same repository, it does not match the advisory-described vulnerability in the /api/upload endpoint.

selected_finding.title: Unauthenticated stored cross-site scripting via SVG file upload

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The finding identifies the exact vulnerability described in the advisory: unauthenticated file upload via POST /api/upload endpoint. The finding correctly identifies the missing authentication middleware (no secureFnc), the vulnerable code location (server/api/projects/index.js:193), and the file upload without sanitization. However, PoC execution failed due to network connectivity issues preventing repository cloning, preventing runtime verification of the exploit success or bug activation predicates.
evidence: Finding correctly identifies POST /api/upload as the vulnerable endpoint matching the advisory.
Finding correctly identifies missing authentication (no secureFnc middleware) as the root cause.
Finding correctly identifies code location server/api/projects/index.js:193 matching advisory reference.
Finding demonstrates understanding of unrestricted file upload vulnerability class (CWE-434).
PoC execution attempted but failed due to network connectivity failure during git clone step.
blockers_to_higher_tier: Network connectivity failure prevented git clone of FUXA repository from GitHub.
Unable to verify runtime exploit success predicate (successful unauthenticated file upload with XSS payload served intact).
Unable to verify runtime bug activation predicate (unauthenticated request accepted by /api/upload endpoint).

poc_executions.observed_effect: PoC execution failed during git clone step due to network connectivity failure: 'Failed to connect to github.com port 443 after 54929 ms: Couldn't connect to server'. The PoC attempted to clone the FUXA repository from GitHub but could not establish a network connection.
